name: Internal Combustion Engine
tagline: Chemical energy released inside cylinders, pushing pistons that turn a crankshaft into useful rotary power
problem:
Steam engines are powerful but carry boiler inertia: slow start, heavy plant, and continuous need for water treatment and fire-tending. Many mobile applications wanted power dense enough to fit into a carriage or airframe: quick start, high rpm, and mechanical output without a separate boiler building.

Internal combustion answers by oxidising fuel inside the working cylinders themselves. The chemical reaction products become the working fluid that pushes pistons directly. That raises power-to-weight and enables minutes-to-full-power — critical for vehicles and small workshops.

The obstacles are precision and control: fuel must meter correctly with air; ignition must time near top dead centre; materials must survive pressure peaks and thermal cycling; lubrication must work while combustion byproducts contaminate oil. Each subsystem is a failure mode: lean knock, rich fouling, valve float, bearing wipe.
overview:
A four-stroke petrol engine repeats: intake (draw air-fuel), compression, power (spark ignites mixture, expansion pushes piston), exhaust (scavenge burnt gas). The crankshaft converts reciprocation to rotation; a flywheel stores energy between power strokes; a camshaft times valve opening via lifters and springs or overhead mechanisms.

Fuel systems evolved from surface carburettors to venturi carburettors to port fuel injection to direct injection — each improves metering, emissions, or knock resistance. Ignition moved from flame gaps to magnetos to battery-coil distributor to electronic coil-on-plug. Lubrication is pressure-fed from a pump through galleries, with splash for some components; oil filters extend life.

Cooling removes heat that does not become shaft work — liquid jackets with radiator or air-cooled fins. Exhaust systems route hot gases safely and attenuate noise; catalytic converters oxidise pollutants when supported by oxygen sensors and three-way chemistry.

Diesel variants compress air until hot, then inject fuel to self-ignite — higher compression, different injection pressures, no spark plug — sharing many mechanical subsystems with petrol engines.

principles.name: Otto cycle (idealised)
principles.explanation: Four-stroke spark-ignition engines approximate the Otto cycle: compression raises temperature; rapid combustion approximates heat addition at top dead centre; expansion extracts work; exhaust rejects heat.

principles.name: Knock (detonation)
principles.explanation: End-gas auto-ignition ahead of the flame front produces sharp pressure waves damaging pistons and bearings — controlled by octane, compression ratio, charge cooling, and knock sensors.

principles.name: Mean effective pressure
principles.explanation: Torque scales with displaced volume and how effectively each stroke converts chemical energy to pressure over the piston area — tuning and breathing (valves, ports) dominate.

principles.name: Friction mean effective pressure
principles.explanation: Parasitic losses from rings, bearings, pumps, and accessories consume gross indicated work — why idle fuel consumption exists even at zero output power.

components.name: Cylinder block
components.function: Contains cylinders, coolant passages, sometimes crankcase integration
components.criticalNote: Deck flatness affects head gasket seal — warpage from overheating ruins engines

components.name: Crankshaft
components.madeFrom: Forged steel, nitrided journals in performance engines
components.criticalNote: Oil hole radii must be filleted — stress concentrations snap cranks

components.name: Pistons and rings
components.function: Seals combustion pressure, controls oil film
components.madeFrom: Aluminium pistons, cast iron or steel rings
components.criticalNote: Broken ring lands cause blow-by and oil consumption

components.name: Cylinder head and valvetrain
components.function: Admits charge and exhausts burnt gas with timed lift profiles
components.madeFrom: Aluminium or cast iron head, steel valves, springs
components.criticalNote: Interference engines bend valves if timing belt fails — catastrophic

components.name: Ignition system
components.function: Creates spark at commanded crank angle
components.criticalNote: Spark energy must exceed misfire threshold under boost and dilution

components.name: Fuel metering
components.criticalNote: Dirt in fuel destroys injector seats — filtration is reliability

components.name: Oil pump and filtration
components.madeFrom: Gear or gerotor pump, pleated filter media
components.criticalNote: Oil starvation on long corners with low sump — baffling matters in vehicles

components.name: Cooling system
components.criticalNote: Air pockets cause local boiling — steam pockets collapse impellers

rawMaterials.name: Alloy steels
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Imported stock until rolling and heat treat exist

rawMaterials.name: Aluminium
rawMaterials.purpose: Lightweight heads and pistons in many designs
earthLocations: Bauxite belts refined by Hall-Héroult where electricity exists

rawMaterials.name: Petrol or diesel
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Methanol, ethanol, hydrogen carriers — require engine/fuel-system matching

buildSteps.title: Blueprint and tolerance stack
buildSteps.description: Define bore, stroke, compression ratio, bearing clearances, valve timing diagram. Stack tolerances for worst-case valve-to-piston and head gasket crush.
buildSteps.warningNote: Amateur high-compression builds without knock sensing are destructive

buildSteps.title: Machine block and crank
buildSteps.description: Bore/hone cylinders to roundness with crosshatch for ring bedding; grind crank journals to size; balance rotating assembly.
buildSteps.warningNote: Cast iron dust is hazardous — ventilation

buildSteps.title: Assemble bottom end
buildSteps.description: Install mains with specified crush and clearance; torque in stages; verify crank spins freely; check end float.

buildSteps.title: Assemble top end and time valvetrain
buildSteps.description: Set cam timing per marks; verify interference clearance; adjust valve lash if solid lifters; torque head in spiral pattern.
buildSteps.warningNote: Reused head bolts may yield — replace if torque-to-yield design

buildSteps.title: Prime oil, first start, break-in
buildSteps.description: Pre-lube galleries; disable fuel/spark for initial crank; establish oil pressure; start with conservative load and varying rpm per cam/bore finish requirements.
buildSteps.warningNote: Fuel leaks plus hot exhaust ignite — keep extinguisher aimed

history.event: Étienne Lenoir builds a practical gas-driven internal combustion engine, demonstrating serial production concepts.
Nikolaus Otto's four-stroke patent frames the dominant automotive thermodynamic architecture.
Gottlieb Daimler and Wilhelm Maybach develop compact high-speed petrol engines enabling vehicles and early aircraft experiments.

impact:
Internal combustion engines personalised mechanised motion: cars, motorcycles, aircraft, tractors, generators. They also anchored petroleum geopolitics and urban air quality challenges — the technology is morally double-edged.

For recovery, rebuilding engines is a mid-industrial skill: foundry, machining, fuel chemistry, and metrology together — not a cottage industry alone.

sources.title: U.S. EPA — Transportation and air quality program index
sources.note: Emissions science and regulatory context for ICE vehicles

sources.title: NIST SP 811
sources.note: SI units for power, energy, and efficiency calculations
